google_cloud_guide: It looks like you don't have Google OAuth credentials yet.
Follow these steps to create them:
2. Click 'Select a project' at the top, then 'New Project'
3. Give it a name (e.g., 'BC Free Flight') and click 'Create'
4. Make sure your new project is selected at the top
5. In the left sidebar, go to 'APIs & Services' > 'Library'
6. Search for 'YouTube Data API v3' and click 'Enable'
7. In the sidebar, go to 'APIs & Services' > 'OAuth consent screen'
- Fill in the app name and your email address
- Click through the remaining steps (scopes, test users)
- Add your Google account as a test user
8. In the sidebar, go to 'APIs & Services' > 'Credentials'
10. Select 'Desktop app' as the application type
12. Copy the 'Client ID' and 'Client Secret' from the popup
youtube_stream_guide: To find your YouTube stream key and URLs:
2. Click 'Go Live' or the camera icon > 'Go Live'
5. The Stream URL and Backup URL are shown below the key
broadcast_title_guide: The broadcast title is updated each time --start runs.
Use {date} anywhere in the title to insert today's date (ISO format).
cron_guide: Cron jobs will automatically start and stop your stream on a schedule.
A cron expression has five fields: minute, hour, day, month, weekday.
30 6 1-31 4-10 *    = every day at 6:30 AM, April through October
For help building cron expressions, visit: https://crontab.guru/

prompts.rtspUrl: RTSP URL of the camera stream
prompts.broadcastId: Existing broadcast ID (leave empty to create new)

messages.oauth_starting: Starting OAuth flow — a browser window will open...

errors.missing_config: Missing YouTube config: {fields}. Run 'python3 stream.py --install' first.
errors.broadcast_complete: Broadcast {broadcast_id} is in 'complete' state and cannot be restarted. Run 'python3 stream.py --install' to create a new broadcast.
